observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
1.13.1 The Office of the Municipal Treasurer update its records yearly
based on assessment roll received from the Assessor’s Office and
prepare and submit updated list of names of taxpayers and the
amount to be collected for the year to the Accounting Office on or
before January 31 of each year; and
1.13.2 The Municipal Accountant prepare an entry in the books at the
beginning of the year based on the updated list of taxpayers
submitted by the Treasurer’s Office in compliance of the New
Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs Volume I.

2.9 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
2.9.1 The Accounting Office prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the
Construction in Progress-Buildings and Other Structures account
in the Trust Fund books in compliance with the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Volume I.
Suggested adjusting entries:
General Fund Books:
Debit – Government Equity xxx
Credit - Construction in Progress-Building
and Other Structures xxx
To correct/transfer CIP account pertaining to
trust fund
Trust Fund books:
Debit - Construction in Progress-Building
and Other Structures xxx
Credit – Due to NGAs xxx
To record construction in progress in the trust fund books and restore
liability account
2.9.2 The Accounting Office comply with the proper recognition of
accounts and recording of transactions in the Trust Fund books and
stop the practice of extinguishing the liability, Due to NGAs
account, upon partial payment of the project, instead see to it that a
proper liquidation, either partial or full, has been made first
before derecognition of Due NGAs or Trust Liabilities account in
compliance with the existing rules and regulations.

3.10 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
3.10.1 Every Department Office of the LGU conduct physical count of PPE
that is existing and found in their office and prepare a report
following the RPCPPE format;
3.10.2 The property officer collect/gather the RPCPPE prepared per office
and consolidate the result in the RPCPPE of the Municipality; and
3.10.3 The Accounting Office reconcile the data gathered with its records
before the issuance of the financial statements and submit a copy of
the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31
of each year in compliance with the New Government Accounting
System Manual for Local Government Unit, Volume I.

4.10 We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the
Acting Municipal Treasurer conduct physical inventory of supplies and
materials and submit the inventory reports to the Accounting Office for
reconciliation and to COA for audit. Also, direct the Municipal Treasurer
to maintain a stock card for its supplies and materials inventory in
compliance with Section 124 of the New Government Accounting Manual
for LGUs, Volume I.

5.9 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting and
Treasurer’s Office:
5.9.1 Transfer the unexpended QRF and MDRR-MOOE balance as of
December 31, 2024 of the total MDRRM Fund to Trust Fund Account
in compliance with the existing COA rules and regulations; and
5.9.2 Recognize the transfer of MDRRM Fund in the Trust Fund books
following the suggested journal entries below:
General Fund books:
Trust Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund xxx
Cash in Bank-LCCA xxx
To record transfer from the general fund of unspent DRRM Fund
Trust Fund books:
Cash in Bank-LCCA xxx
Trust Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund xxx
To record receipts from the general fund of unspent DRRM Fund

7.7 We recommended and the Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point
comply with the preparation and submission of GAD Accomplishment Report
and furnished the Office of the Auditor a copy within five (5) working days
from the end of January of the preceding year in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the provisions of Republic Act 7160 on enforcing collection of interest on unpaid real property taxes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

a. Always require the Municipal Engineer to provide the State Auditors with a list of ongoing 20% DF projects for proper monitoring on the implementation; and

b. Inform COA within 10 days after the awarding of the project contract, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer.
observations.reason: COA was not informed of some construction projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the guidelines and procedures on submission of perfected contracts and Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents to State Auditors within five (5) days in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. We also recommended and the Management agreed to get hold and study the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2021-006 dated September 6, 2021 (Guidelines on the use of Electronic Documents, Electronic Signatures, and Digital Signatures in Government Transactions), as one of the alternatives to the physical submission of accounts to COA.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that programs, projects and activities for children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 be implemented.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management complete the physical count of the Municipality’s PPE, consolidate gathered data in the RPCPPE and reconcile its balances with the accounting records.  Henceforth, submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year for evaluation.

We recommended that the Management reassess the viability and feasibility of the unimplemented PPAs particularly on the procurement and implementation aspects considering the current situation of our country. If found feasible, utilize the fund for the intended projects in the ensuing year, otherwise, realign or reprogram the fund for PPAs that can contribute to the attainment of reducing disaster risks and strengthening the capacity of the LGU in compliance with RA 10121.  Henceforth, the appropriation of MDRRMF shall be well planned considering all the factors affecting the timely implementation of PPAs.
